# Reference Sites Report: Westmag

*Generated by `inspiration-scout` skill — feeds into `design-dna-extractor` +
`layout-innovator` for the v3 RevolutionSection + TeamSection variants.*

## Brief Summary

- **Industry:** Industrial hardware — BLDC motors, drone motors, robot
  actuators. American Dynamism / reshoring positioning. Backed by a16z +
  Founders Fund.
- **Mood:** Industrial, confident, mission-driven. Hardware-rich messaging,
  no SaaS gradients, no soft pastels.
- **Audience:** Drone/robot OEMs (commercial + defense), top engineering
  talent, a16z LPs and the wider American Dynamism investor community.
- **Existing design constraints:** Inter outlined uppercase (w/
  `-webkit-text-stroke`) + IBM Plex Mono body, single orange accent
  `#ff3905`, grainy bg textures, angled clip-path buttons (24px cut),
  Framer Motion fade-in-y at `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`. Dark + light
  section alternation.
- **Sections to iterate:** (1) RevolutionSection (8 variants), (2)
  TeamSection (3–4 variants).
- **Hard "do not include":** SaaS aesthetics, pastel gradients, soft UI,
  WebGL-heavy interactions (too costly + slow load).

---

## Reference 1: Hadrian

- **URL:** https://hadrian.co
- **Overall Match:** 5/5
- **Layout takeaway:** Asymmetric, mission-action layout. Opens with a
  centered hero statement ("Factories of the Future to Reindustrialize
  America"), then left-aligns service tiers as modular content blocks,
  with repeated CTA banners ("Manufacture the future at Hadrian") slicing
  between sections. Apply this to Revolution: 3-point narrative as 3
  modular blocks with full-width contextual banners between.
- **Typography takeaway:** Bold statement-driven headlines paired with
  clean functional body text. Headlines are *declarative* and
  *action-oriented* ("The Future of American Production"), not
  descriptive. Apply: rewrite Revolution sub-headings as declarations.
- **Motion takeaway:** Action-oriented section transitions; no flashy
  scroll-jacking. Restrained, purposeful.
- **Color takeaway:** Neutral monochromatic foundation with location data
  treated as visual accents (CA, AZ, AL, DC chips). Westmag should use
  South San Francisco / domestic supply chain similarly.
- **What to steal:** The "Reindustrialize America" verbal-visual pairing
  — large declarative headline + immediately actionable supporting copy.
  This is the closest analog to what the "America's the sum of its parts"
  section is *trying* to do.
- **What to skip:** Hadrian's service-tier breakdown (precision /
  manufacturing-as-a-service / factories-as-a-service) isn't relevant —
  Westmag's narrative is product-led not service-led.

## Reference 2: K2 Space

- **URL:** https://k2space.com
- **Overall Match:** 4.5/5
- **Layout takeaway:** "Build Bigger" tagline anchors visual hierarchy
  with **comparative side-by-side visuals** (200kg/2kW vs. 3000kg/20kW
  satellites) immediately communicating differentiation. Apply: Revolution
  variant that shows a US motor vs. overseas motor scale or supply-chain
  diagram side-by-side to make the "make them here" point visceral.
- **Typography takeaway:** Bold, minimalist, large sans-serif headers
  emphasizing scale and capability. Top-aligned navigation, vertical
  section flow. Aligned with Westmag's existing Inter outlined uppercase.
- **Motion takeaway:** **Corner flourishes and arrows create directional
  movement** — guides the eye between sections suggesting forward
  momentum. Lightweight to implement with SVG + Framer.
- **Color takeaway:** Dark navy/black backgrounds dominate; white text +
  accent imagery. Westmag already alternates dark/light — could lean
  *darker* on the Revolution section to differentiate from the grainy
  cream of adjacent sections.
- **What to steal:** **CTO quote as visual centerpiece** ("The industry
  went small. We asked: what happens if you go the other way?"). Westmag
  could do the same with a founder quote that anchors the manifesto
  visually. Also: the comparative-visuals device for the "made here vs.
  made overseas" framing.
- **What to skip:** The orbital LEO/MEO/GEO interactive tabs — too
  specific to space, and would conflict with Westmag's simpler 3-point
  scroll narrative.

## Reference 3: Mach Industries

- **URL:** https://machindustries.com
- **Overall Match:** 4/5
- **Layout takeaway:** **Numbered product sections (01–05) create visual
  rhythm.** Mission positioned as *centered substantial block text below
  the hero*, not in the hero itself — gives it gravitas without competing
  for hero attention. Apply: a Revolution variant that uses numbered
  cards (01, 02, 03) for the three points, mission anchored below.
- **Typography takeaway:** Bold weight for headlines, product names
  (Viper, Glide, Stratos, Pike, Dart) as focal points via *size and
  contrast*, not color. Aligned with Westmag's outlined-uppercase
  approach.
- **Motion takeaway:** Implicit hover states; progressive disclosure
  (mission → products → news). Restrained — appropriate for the
  industrial space.
- **Color takeaway:** Monochromatic black/white with accent imagery
  only. Decorative aviation-inspired grid patterns as subtle background
  elements. Westmag could use a similar subtle motor-schematic grid as
  background texture (replacing or in addition to current grain).
- **What to steal:** **Numbered 01/02/03 rhythm** for the three Revolution
  points + the mission-as-centered-block placement.
- **What to skip:** Aviation-specific geometric forms — Westmag is
  motors/robots, not aviation. Use motor-cross-section or
  drone/robot-silhouette grid instead.

## Reference 4: Saronic

- **URL:** https://www.saronic.com
- **Overall Match:** 4/5
- **Layout takeaway:** Hero centers a tight declarative headline
  ("Redefining maritime superiority"); seven numbered capabilities
  (**001–007**) arranged in a tab/carousel below. Staggered vessel
  imagery (Corsair, Marauder) interspersed between content blocks.
- **Typography takeaway:** Clean modern sans-serif with high contrast on
  dark backgrounds. Military nomenclature treated typographically
  (specs, callouts) — could inform how Westmag presents motor specs
  (diameter ranges, KV, stator dimensions).
- **Motion takeaway:** Carousel/tab pattern for numbered capabilities
  suggests **horizontal interaction** — could be a Revolution variant
  where the 3 points are tabs rather than vertical stack.
- **Color takeaway:** Dark background + white text + military-grade
  contrast. Headline-sized number cards (001, 002, 003).
- **What to steal:** The **001/002/003 numbered card pattern** as an
  alternative to Mach's 01/02/03. Could be a Revolution variant with
  three tabbed numbered cards.
- **What to skip:** The seven-item carousel — Westmag has exactly 3
  points to make. More than 3 dilutes the message.

---

## Recommended Priority for Layout Generation

1. **Hadrian** — *primary reference* for the Revolution variant DNA.
   Closest brand positioning match, most directly applicable manifesto
   pattern.
2. **K2 Space** — *secondary* for the comparative-visuals device and
   founder-quote-as-centerpiece pattern. Use for at least 2 of the 8
   Revolution variants.
3. **Mach Industries** — *tertiary* for the numbered-rhythm pattern (01,
   02, 03). Use for at least 2 more Revolution variants.
4. **Saronic** — *contrast reference* for an interactive tab/carousel
   Revolution variant (1 of 8).

## Variant direction matrix (suggested 8 Revolution variants × 4 Team variants)

### RevolutionSection (8 directions):

| # | Direction | Primary inspiration | Theme |
|---|---|---|---|
| A | **Three stacked text blocks** (current v1/v2, no rework) | — | Baseline / control |
| B | **Manifesto banner with cut-in service-tier modules** | Hadrian | Action-oriented |
| C | **Numbered 01/02/03 cards in horizontal row** | Mach | Visual rhythm |
| D | **Side-by-side comparative diagram** (American supply vs. overseas) | K2 Space | Visceral differentiation |
| E | **Founder quote-anchored mission with 3 supporting beats below** | K2 Space | Personality + gravitas |
| F | **Three tabbed numbered panels (001/002/003)** | Saronic | Interactive |
| G | **Full-bleed dark section with corner-flourish chevrons** | K2 Space | Forward-momentum mood |
| H | **Section deleted entirely** — content folded into adjacent sections | (Matt's "easy button") | Minimalism / control test |

### TeamSection (4 directions):

| # | Direction | Theme |
|---|---|---|
| A | **Current v2 layout** (cards + orange wipe-up hover) | Baseline |
| B | **Inline roles list + small bios right + social links** (per ADVISOR `[38:23]`) | Recruiting-forward |
| C | **Daft-Punk-style robot avatars** + bio cards | Personality / brand-distinctive |
| D | **Text-only headline + inline roles grid only** (no member photos/cards) | Minimalist |

---

## Notes on what couldn't be fetched

- **Anduril** (https://www.anduril.com) — JS-rendered, WebFetch returned
  only metadata. Anduril is the most prominent American Dynamism site,
  worth a manual visual review if anyone has time. Likely informs both
  the dark-mode-defense aesthetic and the editorial-mission-statement
  pattern, similar to what we've drawn from Hadrian + K2.
- **Shield AI** — certificate expired during fetch. Skipping.
- **Apptronik / Boom / Figure** — useful supporting context but not
  selected as top-4 references; they overlap with the Hadrian / K2 /
  Mach / Saronic recommendations on relevant patterns.
